project_identity.course_context: Final NLP project with phased academic deliverables
project_identity.current_overall_phase: Between proposal/planning and early implementation-testing preparation
remembered_constraints: Project follows 4 phases: Literature Review, Proposal (ACL format), Implementation, Experiments
Prefer advanced topics such as multimodal, graph-based knowledge, agentic AI, cultural chatbot, or similarly substantial NLP work
Avoid overly simple projects such as plain RAG, simple sentiment analysis, or generic prompting-only work

core_project_direction.working_problem_area: Understanding implied meaning, pragmatic interpretation, and context-sensitive language understanding for Burmese
motivation: Pragmatic meaning is harder than literal meaning
Many models perform poorly when context, intent, and implied meaning matter
The project aims to show measurable improvement rather than relying only on human judgments
high_level_modeling_direction: A staged progression from weaker baselines to a fuller context-aware system
Potential progression discussed: zero-shot -> meaning-only -> context-aware -> full model
Possible use of fine-tuning rather than only prompting
Need mathematical/quantitative proof that the model works

main_problem_statement.summary: The project aims to build and evaluate a Burmese NLP system that can better interpret context-dependent or pragmatic meaning, rather than only literal surface meaning.
problems_to_tackle: Difficulty of representing pragmatic meaning and context
Need for a dataset that is sufficient and aligned with the task
Need for strong baselines to prove improvement
Need for evaluation that is rigorous and quantitative
Need to keep the work within course scope while still being advanced enough for acceptance
why_this_is_hard: Pragmatics is harder to annotate and evaluate than direct classification
Need to connect literature, dataset design, modeling, and experiments into one coherent research story
Need to justify methodology to a professor who may not be focused on Burmese specifically

primary_goals: Design a research-worthy NLP project around Burmese context/pragmatics
Build a system that improves over simple baselines
Show empirical evidence of improvement with quantitative metrics
Produce all required course deliverables in ACL-style academic format
secondary_goals: Keep the topic advanced enough to be accepted by the professor
Ground the project in ACL-only literature
Create a workflow that is explainable in papers, slides, and presentation diagrams
Make the methodology teachable and presentable to teammates
success_criteria: Sufficient dataset for the chosen scope
Strong literature review from ACL Anthology
Evaluation with measurable metrics rather than only anecdotal examples

topic_selection_stage.status: Discussed and narrowed toward Burmese pragmatic/context-aware NLP
notes: The user liked the fine-tuning direction
The project should not be a simple or generic idea
It should feel substantial enough for a final NLP project

requirements: Total of 16 papers for a 4-member team
notes: Relevant papers can include Burmese, structurally similar low-resource languages, and English pragmatics/context papers if they directly support the scope
The literature should connect to low-resource NLP, context-aware understanding, pragmatics, or fine-tuning approaches

deliverables_discussed: Workflow diagram placement in the paper
Slides explanation and how to visually present model progression
notes: The paper is being prepared in ACL LaTeX format
The user asked where to place diagrams in the paper
The user also requested presentation-friendly text for model diagrams

implementation_stage.status: Started in practical terms but not finished end-to-end
notes: Training has been run at least in an early setup
A runtime/device issue was debugged and explained
The user explicitly confirmed that the project has not tackled the whole thing yet
The user asked whether they are now in the testing stage
Clarification given earlier: not the whole project yet; only part of the pipeline has been exercised

modeling_ideas: Fine-tuning a model rather than using only zero-shot prompting
Comparing simpler and stronger model settings
Using context as an explicit signal in the full model
Potential classifier component for evaluation and measurable performance proof

progression_for_experiments.purpose: Model focuses on literal meaning or local text without richer context
Model uses surrounding information and pragmatic cues
Final proposed system with the complete method

presentation_support.diagram_need: A clear left-box/right-box style diagram for slides and paper
presentation_support.example_need: Use Burmese sentence examples to illustrate increasing interpretation quality across model stages

known_discussions: Whether the dataset is enough for the project scope
Need to align dataset design with the exact task
Need for measurable outputs and not only subjective interpretation
open_dataset_questions: Final dataset source has not been locked in the remembered discussion here
Need to define annotation/unit of analysis clearly
Need to ensure class/task balance if using classification
Need train/validation/test splits consistent with research evaluation
data_requirements: Labels or supervision design that match the research question
Enough examples to compare baseline and proposed method

evaluation_plan.core_expectation: Use quantitative evaluation to prove the method works
discussed_evaluation_needs: Mathematical proof in the sense of measurable model performance
Avoid relying only on human judgment
additional_experiment_needs: Ablation or staged comparison across baseline variants
Error analysis with representative Burmese examples

project_assets_discussed: GitHub README up to proposal stage
Team-facing summary/report explaining the topic and workflow

training_run_observed.general_interpretation: The loss decreased across epochs, suggesting the run was functioning and learning to some extent.

runtime_debugging_discussion.issue: A device/runtime-related setup issue was discussed around GPU vs CPU execution/testing.
runtime_debugging_discussion.user_concern: The user was on a GitHub-connected T4 runtime and had not manually changed anything.
important_takeaway: Part of the debugging involved explaining why the code/runtime behavior changed
CPU testing was used as a diagnostic step rather than as the intended full training environment

current_status_interpretation: A partial training/test workflow has been exercised
This does not mean the whole research pipeline is complete
The project still needs the remaining end-to-end components, experiments, and polished evaluation

what_is_done_so_far: A promising Burmese pragmatics/context-aware project direction has been identified
The scope was shaped to be advanced and course-appropriate
ACL-only literature review requirement was established
Proposal components such as title/abstract/introduction were discussed
ACL LaTeX structure and figure placement were discussed
Presentation framing for the model progression was discussed
At least one early training run was completed
A runtime/debugging issue related to execution environment was discussed and addressed
what_is_not_done_yet: Final locked task definition with exact labels and dataset specification
Complete literature table with all required ACL papers
Full implementation of the final proposed method

open_problems_and_risks.why_it_matters: Pragmatics can become too broad if the task is not sharply defined.
An advanced idea may fail if the data is too small or weakly aligned.
Without a clear metric-based evaluation, the project may look vague.
The topic must look academically strong but still feasible.
A full context-aware system may require more engineering than originally expected.

phase_1_lock_scope: Confirm whether the task is classification, ranking, or another supervised framing
Write a one-paragraph final problem statement
phase_2_finalize_literature: Prioritize low-resource, pragmatics, context modeling, and fine-tuning studies
Create a structured literature review table
Extract datasets, methods, and evaluation metrics from each paper
phase_3_dataset_design: Finalize dataset source or construction strategy
Check class balance and annotation consistency
phase_4_build_baselines: Keep evaluation pipeline identical across settings
phase_5_build_final_model: Document all hyperparameters and runtime settings
phase_6_experiments_and_analysis: Show representative Burmese examples where context changes meaning
phase_7_writing_and_presentation: Prepare slide storyline from motivation -> method -> results -> examples -> conclusion
Make sure every claim in slides is supported by results

current_best_understanding_of_status.plain_language_summary: The project is no longer just an idea, but it is also not complete. The team has a strong direction, proposal-related material, and some early implementation/testing activity, but still needs to finalize the exact task, lock the dataset/evaluation design, finish the full method, and run complete experiments.
current_best_understanding_of_status.are_we_in_testing_stage: Partially/early, but not in the sense of having completed the whole project.

uncertainties_or_missing_details: Exact final dataset name and schema are not fully established in the remembered discussion provided here
